sentence_reviews.text: Paired views or examples define similarity signals that shape a representation space.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44; CLIP abstract lines 42-43.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both CPC and CLIP use paired or contrasting examples to learn representations through similarity/contrast signals.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Contrastive learning, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Contrastive and mutual-information objectives is modeled as depending on Contrastive learning, Information theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Multimodal foundation models, Dense vector retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
